original_image_tags: Aged, off-white document paper with uneven edges and two punch holes at the top. The primary content consists of multiple FBI reception and routing stamps and a handwritten note at the bottom. Faint bleed-through text from the front side of the page is visible, including the handwritten words "Edwin J. Lopez" reversed and backwards at the top right.

elements.description: Back of a document featuring multiple ink stamps and handwritten annotations. Visible stamps include 'RECEIVED - NICHOLS F.B.I. U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE' dated JUL 6 9 33 AM '53, another identical stamp inverted, 'REC'D RECORDS SEC', and 'RECEIVED - DIRECTOR F.B.I. U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE' with dates JUL 3, JUL 8, and JUL 10. Handwritten text in cursive reads 'Duro Textiles' and '7-3-53', along with typed 'MR JONES'. The document has two hole-punches at the top.

judge.reason: Both agree on the primary visual content (ink stamps, handwritten notes, hole punches), but Description B adds specific quoted text for stamps and handwriting that Description A does not mention, creating a detail mismatch without direct contradiction on verbatim text.
